name: Clovis I
role: King of the Franks (481–511 CE) — Founder of Merovingian dynasty
quote: I, who have conquered many nations, cannot bear that those heretics should reign over part of the Gauls.

name: Charlemagne
role: King of the Franks & Holy Roman Emperor (800–814 CE) — Father of Europe
quote: To have another language is to possess a second soul.

name: Joan of Arc
role: Military leader and national heroine (1412–1431) — Patron saint of France
quote: I am not afraid; I was born to do this.

name: Cardinal Richelieu
role: Chief Minister under Louis XIII (1624–1642) — Architect of French absolutism
quote: Give me six lines written by the most honorable of men, and I will find an excuse in them to hang him.

name: Louis XIV
role: King of France (1643–1715) — The Sun King; absolute monarch
quote: L'État, c'est moi. (I am the State.)

name: Marie Antoinette
role: Queen of France (1774–1792) — Symbol of Ancien Régime excess
quote: Courage! I have shown it for years; think you I shall lose it at the moment when my sufferings are to end?

name: Maximilien Robespierre
role: Revolutionary leader, Committee of Public Safety (1793–1794) — The Incorruptible
quote: The secret of freedom lies in educating people, whereas the secret of tyranny is keeping them ignorant.

name: Napoleon Bonaparte
role: Emperor of the French (1804–1814, 1815) — Military genius and lawgiver
quote: Impossible is a word found only in the dictionary of fools.

name: Émile Zola
role: Novelist and public intellectual (1840–1902) — Author of J'accuse
quote: If you shut up truth and bury it under the ground, it will but grow, and gather to itself such explosive power that the day it bursts through it will blow up everything in its way.

name: Léon Blum
role: Prime Minister (1936–1937, 1938, 1946) — Leader of Popular Front government
quote: My ideal is the honest man, the man who believes in justice and who fights for it.

name: Philippe Pétain
role: Marshal and Head of Vichy State (1940–1944) — Hero of Verdun, traitor of the Republic
quote: I make to France the gift of my person to attenuate her misfortune.

name: Charles de Gaulle
role: President of the Fifth Republic (1959–1969) — Leader of Free France in WWII
quote: France cannot be France without greatness.

name: Simone de Beauvoir
role: Philosopher and feminist author (1908–1986) — Author of The Second Sex
quote: One is not born, but rather becomes, a woman.

name: François Mitterrand
role: President of France (1981–1995) — First Socialist president of Fifth Republic
quote: France is a country that produces 246 different kinds of cheese — a country that cannot be governed.

name: Jean-Marie Le Pen
role: Founder of Front National (1972–2011) — Leader of French far-right for four decades
quote: The French were given a choice between pestilence and cholera — they chose cholera.

name: Marine Le Pen
role: President of Rassemblement National (2011–) — Far-right leader, two-time presidential runner-up
quote: France is no longer France. We must give it back to the French.

name: Nicolas Sarkozy
role: President of France (2007–2012) — Later convicted of corruption
quote: Work more to earn more — France is back!

name: Emmanuel Macron
role: President of France (2017–) — Youngest president in French history
quote: I want to be the president who re-launches a deep transformation of France.

name: Jean-Luc Mélenchon
role: Leader of La France Insoumise (LFI) — Radical left politician, three-time presidential candidate
quote: The climate emergency, the ecological emergency, the social emergency — these are the only emergencies that matter.

name: Victor Hugo
role: Author, politician, exile (1802–1885) — Author of Les Misérables; senator; exiled by Napoleon III
quote: To love beauty is to see light.
